page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

This server includes 1843 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, making it suitable for workloads requiring fast local scratch space. In benchmark testing, the memory subsystem demonstrates strong performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 230.36 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 111.32 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Conversely, single-core processing tasks show average to weak performance; single-threaded bzip3 decompression scored 8.76 GB/sec, placing in the bottom 25%.

The high memory density and strong large-block memory bandwidth make this instance class cost-efficient for memory-bound applications, though it presents tradeoffs in compute-heavy or sequential processing tasks. Large language model inference benchmarks show weak text generation speeds, such as 7.59 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model. Consequently, the hardware profile is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise resource planning applications, and large-scale data processing workloads rather than intensive sequential CPU tasks or real-time AI generation.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It provides 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage. The instance features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Benchmark results indicate strong memory bandwidth, with large-block uncached reads reaching 230.36 GB/sec and writes at 111.32 GB/sec. However, single-threaded performance is more modest, as seen in weak bzip3 decompression and slow LLM text generation speeds. This resource configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory data stores, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong large-block memory bandwidth for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density, local storage, and strong large-block memory bandwidth for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8 GB per vCPU memory-to-core ratio.
1843 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 230.36 GB/sec.
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
